name: getRelativeStarScale
description: Returns the relative star scale factor. Higher values make brighter stars appear much larger than fainter ones.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var scale = StelSkyDrawer.getRelativeStarScale();
core.debug("Relative star scale: " + scale);

name: setRelativeStarScale
description: Sets the relative star scale factor. Controls how much larger bright stars appear compared to faint stars. Default is 1.0.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Relative scale factor (0.5 = less contrast, 2.0 = more contrast between bright and faint stars).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getAbsoluteStarScale
description: Returns the absolute star brightness scale factor. Affects the overall brightness of all stars.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var absScale = StelSkyDrawer.getAbsoluteStarScale();
core.debug("Absolute star scale: " + absScale);

name: setAbsoluteStarScale
description: Sets the absolute star brightness scale factor. Affects the overall brightness of all stars.

parameters.name: scale
parameters.description: Absolute scale factor (0.5 = dimmer stars, 2.0 = brighter stars).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getTwinkleAmount
description: Returns the amount of star twinkling (scintillation) effect. Higher values cause more pronounced twinkling.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var twinkle = StelSkyDrawer.getTwinkleAmount();
core.debug("Twinkle amount: " + twinkle);

name: setTwinkleAmount
description: Sets the amount of star twinkling (scintillation) effect. 0 = no twinkling, 1 = maximum twinkling.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagTwinkle
description: Returns whether star twinkling (scintillation) is enabled.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagTwinkle
description: Enables or disables star twinkling (scintillation) effect.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagForcedTwinkle
description: Returns whether forced twinkling is enabled (twinkling even when the atmosphere is disabled). Useful for planetarium shows.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagForcedTwinkle
description: Enables or disables forced twinkling (twinkling even when the atmosphere is turned off).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
// Force twinkling even without atmosphere
StelSkyDrawer.setFlagForcedTwinkle(true);

name: getLightPollutionLuminance
description: Returns the current light pollution luminance in cd/m². Higher values simulate more light pollution, showing fewer stars.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var lum = StelSkyDrawer.getLightPollutionLuminance();
core.debug("Light pollution: " + lum + " cd/m²");

name: setLightPollutionLuminance
description: Sets the light pollution luminance. Higher values simulate urban skies with fewer visible stars. Values typically range from 0.0001 (pristine dark sky) to 0.01 (urban).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getBortleScaleIndex
description:
⚠️ **WARNING:** This is a `public` method, not a `slot`. It may not work reliably in scripts.

Returns the Bortle Dark-Sky Scale index (1 to 9) based on the current light pollution luminance. 1 = excellent dark sky, 9 = inner-city sky.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method. Its reliability in scripts is not guaranteed.

example:
// May not work reliably:
// var bortle = StelSkyDrawer.getBortleScaleIndex();

name: getFlagDrawBigStarHalo
description: Returns whether a large halo is drawn around very bright stars.
returnDescription: `true` if big star halo is enabled.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagDrawBigStarHalo
description: Enables or disables large halos around very bright stars (like Sirius, Vega, etc.).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagStarSpiky
description: Returns whether stars are drawn with diffraction spikes (rays).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagStarSpiky
description: Enables or disables diffraction spikes (rays) on bright stars.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getLimitMagnitude
description:
⚠️ **WARNING:** This is a `public` method, not a `slot`. It may not work reliably in scripts.

Returns the current limiting magnitude for point sources (stars). Stars fainter than this are not drawn.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method. Its reliability in scripts is not guaranteed.

example:
// May not work reliably:
// var limit = StelSkyDrawer.getLimitMagnitude();

name: getFlagStarMagnitudeLimit
description: Returns whether the user-defined star magnitude limit is active. Use `core.getStelProperty('StelSkyDrawer.flagStarMagnitudeLimit')` as an alternative.
returnDescription: `true` if star magnitude limit is active.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagStarMagnitudeLimit
description: Enables or disables the user-defined star magnitude limit. When enabled, stars fainter than `customStarMagLimit` are not drawn.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagNebulaMagnitudeLimit
description: Returns whether the user-defined deep-sky object magnitude limit is active. Use `core.getStelProperty('StelSkyDrawer.flagNebulaMagnitudeLimit')` as an alternative.
returnDescription: `true` if nebula magnitude limit is active.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagNebulaMagnitudeLimit
description: Enables or disables the user-defined deep-sky object magnitude limit.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagPlanetMagnitudeLimit
description: Returns whether the user-defined solar system object magnitude limit is active. Use `core.getStelProperty('StelSkyDrawer.flagPlanetMagnitudeLimit')` as an alternative.
returnDescription: `true` if planet magnitude limit is active.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagPlanetMagnitudeLimit
description: Enables or disables the user-defined solar system object magnitude limit.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getCustomStarMagnitudeLimit
description: Returns the user-defined star magnitude limit value. Use `core.getStelProperty('StelSkyDrawer.customStarMagLimit')` as an alternative.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var starLimit = StelSkyDrawer.getCustomStarMagnitudeLimit();
core.debug("Star limit: " + starLimit);

name: setCustomStarMagnitudeLimit
description: Sets the user-defined star magnitude limit. Stars fainter than this value will not be shown when `flagStarMagnitudeLimit` is enabled.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getCustomNebulaMagnitudeLimit
description: Returns the user-defined deep-sky object magnitude limit value. Use `core.getStelProperty('StelSkyDrawer.customNebulaMagLimit')` as an alternative.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var nebulaLimit = StelSkyDrawer.getCustomNebulaMagnitudeLimit();
core.debug("Nebula limit: " + nebulaLimit);

name: setCustomNebulaMagnitudeLimit
description: Sets the user-defined deep-sky object magnitude limit.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example: StelSkyDrawer.setCustomNebulaMagnitudeLimit(8.5);

name: getCustomPlanetMagnitudeLimit
description: Returns the user-defined solar system object magnitude limit value. Use `core.getStelProperty('StelSkyDrawer.customPlanetMagLimit')` as an alternative.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var planetLimit = StelSkyDrawer.getCustomPlanetMagnitudeLimit();
core.debug("Planet limit: " + planetLimit);

name: setCustomPlanetMagnitudeLimit
description: Sets the user-defined solar system object magnitude limit.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example: StelSkyDrawer.setCustomPlanetMagnitudeLimit(6.5);

name: getLimitLuminance
description:
⚠️ **WARNING:** This is a `public` method, not a `slot`. It may not work reliably in scripts.

Returns the luminance threshold for the faintest visible object.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method. Its reliability in scripts is not guaranteed.

example:
// May not work reliably:
// var limLum = StelSkyDrawer.getLimitLuminance();

name: getFlagLuminanceAdaptation
description: Returns whether luminance adaptation (eye adaptation to brightness) is enabled.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagLuminanceAdaptation
description: Enables or disables luminance adaptation (simulating human eye adaptation to different brightness levels).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getDaylightLabelThreshold
description: Returns the sky brightness threshold for switching text labels to a dark color.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var threshold = StelSkyDrawer.getDaylightLabelThreshold();
core.debug("Label threshold: " + threshold);

name: setDaylightLabelThreshold
description: Sets the sky brightness threshold for switching text labels to a dark color. When the sky is brighter than this, labels become black for better visibility.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getWorldAdaptationLuminance
description:
⚠️ **WARNING:** This is a `public` method, not a `slot`. It may not work reliably in scripts.

Returns the current world adaptation luminance value, representing how bright the scene appears to the simulated eye.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public` method. Its reliability in scripts is not guaranteed.

example:
// May not work reliably:
// var adaptLum = StelSkyDrawer.getWorldAdaptationLuminance();

name: getExtinctionCoefficient
description: Returns the atmospheric extinction coefficient (mag/airmass). Higher values cause stars to dim faster near the horizon.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var extCoeff = StelSkyDrawer.getExtinctionCoefficient();
core.debug("Extinction coefficient: " + extCoeff);

name: setExtinctionCoefficient
description: Sets the atmospheric extinction coefficient. Higher values simulate more atmospheric haze, dimming stars near the horizon.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getAtmosphereTemperature
description: Returns the atmospheric temperature used for refraction calculations (°Celsius).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var temp = StelSkyDrawer.getAtmosphereTemperature();
core.debug("Atmospheric temperature: " + temp + "°C");

name: setAtmosphereTemperature
description: Sets the atmospheric temperature used for refraction calculations. Affects how much stars appear displaced near the horizon.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getAtmospherePressure
description: Returns the atmospheric pressure used for refraction calculations (millibars).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var pressure = StelSkyDrawer.getAtmospherePressure();
core.debug("Atmospheric pressure: " + pressure + " mbar");

name: setAtmospherePressure
description: Sets the atmospheric pressure used for refraction calculations. Higher pressure causes more refraction near the horizon.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: getFlagDrawSunAfterAtmosphere
description: Returns whether the Sun is drawn after the atmosphere layer. Affects how the Sun appears through atmospheric scattering.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagDrawSunAfterAtmosphere
description: Sets whether the Sun is drawn after the atmosphere layer. Can affect the Sun's color and appearance at sunrise/sunset.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example: StelSkyDrawer.setFlagDrawSunAfterAtmosphere(true);

name: getFlagEarlySunHalo
description: Returns whether the Sun's halo is drawn before the Sun's 3D sphere.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagEarlySunHalo
description: Sets whether the Sun's halo is drawn before the Sun's 3D sphere.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example: StelSkyDrawer.setFlagEarlySunHalo(true);

name: getFlagTfromK
description: Returns whether turbidity (T) is computed from the extinction coefficient (k).
returnDescription: `true` if T is computed from k.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

name: setFlagTfromK
description: Sets whether turbidity (T) is computed from the extinction coefficient.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example: StelSkyDrawer.setFlagTfromK(true);

name: getT
description: Returns the atmospheric turbidity value (Preetham sky model parameter). Higher values indicate more haze or pollution.
returnDescription: Turbidity value (2 to 6 typical, 5 is average).

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.

example:
var t = StelSkyDrawer.getT();
core.debug("Turbidity: " + t);

name: setT
description: Sets the atmospheric turbidity value. Higher values simulate more atmospheric haze and pollution, affecting sky color.

compatibility.notes: This is a `public slot` and is safe to use.
